entries.text:
Implement the following plan:

# MeNoach IDE — MVP Implementation Plan

## Context
Building a desktop IDE inspired by QuadCode, starting from an empty directory. The MVP delivers the core IDE skeleton: app shell, file explorer, code editor, terminal, and pixel mascot — the foundation everything else (AI agents, issues, tools system) builds on later.

## Tech Stack
- **Electron** (desktop shell) + **electron-vite** (build)
- **React 18 + TypeScript** (UI)
- **Monaco Editor** (code editing)
- **xterm.js + node-pty** (terminal)
- **Zustand** (state management)
- **react-resizable-panels** (split panes)
- **lucide-react** (icons)
- **CSS Modules + CSS custom properties** (styling)

## Theme — Dark Purple
| Token | Value | Usage |
|---|---|---|
| `--bg-primary` | `#1a1425` | Main background |
| `--bg-secondary` | `#231c30` | Sidebar, panels |
| `--bg-tertiary` | `#2d2440` | Toolbar, headers |
| `--bg-editor` | `#1e1730` | Monaco editor |
| `--border-primary` | `#3d2d5c` | Borders |
| `--accent-purple` | `#8b5cf6` | Primary accent |
| `--accent-cyan` | `#22d3ee` | Status bars |
| `--accent-green` | `#4ade80` | Dev tool buttons |
| `--accent-orange` | `#fb923c` | AI agent buttons |
| `--text-primary` | `#e8e0f0` | Main text |
| `--text-secondary` | `#a090b8` | Muted text |

## Project Structure
```
MeNoach IDE/
├── package.json
├── electron-vite.config.ts
├── tsconfig.json / tsconfig.node.json / tsconfig.web.json
├── .gitignore
├── resources/
│   ├── icon.ico
│   └── mascot/mascot-spritesheet.png
├── src/
│   ├── main/                        # Electron main process
│   │   ├── index.ts                 # BrowserWindow, app lifecycle
│   │   ├── menu.ts                  # Native app menu
│   │   ├── windowState.ts           # Persist window bounds
│   │   ├── ipc/
│   │   │   ├── index.ts             # Register all handlers
│   │   │   ├── fileSystem.ts        # readDirectory, readFile, writeFile
│   │   │   ├── terminal.ts          # PTY create/write/resize/kill
│   │   │   └── dialog.ts            # Native dialogs
│   │   └── services/
│   │       ├── ptyManager.ts        # node-pty instance lifecycle
│   │       └── fileWatcher.ts       # chokidar watcher
│   ├── preload/
│   │   ├── index.ts                 # contextBridge exposing window.api
│   │   └── api.d.ts                 # TypeScript types for window.api
│   └── renderer/
│       ├── index.html
│       ├── main.tsx                  # React root
│       ├── App.tsx
│       ├── assets/fonts/             # JetBrains Mono
│       ├── assets/mascot/            # Sprite sheet
│       ├── styles/
│       │   ├── global.css            # Reset, font-face, root vars
│       │   ├── variables.css         # All CSS custom properties
│       │   └── theme.ts             # TS exports of same tokens
│       ├── components/
│       │   ├── layout/               # AppLayout, PanelLayout
│       │   ├── toolbar/              # Toolbar, ToolsDropdown, LayoutToggle, etc.
│       │   ├── sidebar/              # FileExplorer, FileTree, FileTreeItem
│       │   ├── editor/               # EditorArea, EditorTabs, CodeEditor, Breadcrumb
│       │   ├── terminal/             # TerminalPanel, TerminalInstance, TerminalTabs
│       │   ├── statusbar/            # StatusBar, StatusItem
│       │   ├── mascot/               # MascotWidget, useSpriteAnimation
│       │   └── common/               # IconButton, Dropdown, Badge, Tooltip
│       ├── stores/                   # workspaceStore, fileTreeStore, editorStore,
│       │                               terminalStore, layoutStore, uiStore
│       ├── hooks/                    # useIpc, useFileSystem, useTerminal, etc.
│       ├── types/                    # fileSystem.ts, editor.ts, terminal.ts, ipc.ts
│       └── utils/                    # fileExtensions.ts, pathUtils.ts
```

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Scaffold Project
- `npm create @quick-start/electron` with React+TS template
- Restructure to match plan, install all deps
- Configure `electron-vite.config.ts` (externalize `node-pty`)
- Set up tsconfigs, .gitignore
- Add JetBrains Mono font files
- Create `global.css`, `variables.css`, `theme.ts` with full color system
- Verify `npm run dev` launches blank Electron window

### Step 2: App Shell + Layout System
- Build `AppLayout.tsx` — CSS Grid: toolbar (40px) | body (flex) | statusbar (24px)
- Build `PanelLayout.tsx` — `react-resizable-panels` with sidebar + editor + terminal
- Create `layoutStore.ts` — mode, sidebar/terminal visibility
- Style resize handles with purple accent
- Stub toolbar and statusbar as colored bars

### Step 3: Toolbar
- `AppLogo.tsx` — app icon + "MeNoach IDE" + settings gear
- `ToolsDropdown.tsx` — green button, categorized mega-menu (placeholder items)
- `QuickAccessButtons.tsx` — orange "Claude" + "Codex" buttons (placeholder)
- `LayoutToggle.tsx` — 3 buttons switching `layoutStore.mode`
- `WorkspaceSelector.tsx` — shows folder name, opens native dialog via IPC

### Step 4: File Explorer
- Main process: `ipc/fileSystem.ts` — `readDirectory`, `readFile`, `writeFile`
- Preload bridge: `window.api.fs.*`
- `fileTreeStore.ts` — loadDirectory, toggleDirectory, filter
- `FileExplorer.tsx` — header, filter input, toolbar, tree
- `FileTree.tsx` + `FileTreeItem.tsx` — recursive tree with icons
- `fileIcons.ts` — extension-to-icon/color mapping
- Wire: click file → open in editor, click folder → expand

### Step 5: Code Editor
- `editorStore.ts` — tabs, active tab, content, modified tracking
- `monacoTheme.ts` — register `menoach-dark` theme (red headers, green bold, pink links)
- `EditorTabs.tsx` — tab bar with `</> EDITOR` badge, modified dots, close buttons
- `Breadcrumb.tsx` — file path segments
- `CodeEditor.tsx` — Monaco wrapper with theme, language detection
- `WelcomeTab.tsx` — empty state with logo + shortcuts
- Wire: Ctrl+S saves, Ctrl+W closes tab

### Step 6: Integrated Terminal
- `services/ptyManager.ts` — manages node-pty instances
- `ipc/terminal.ts` — create/write/resize/kill handlers
- `terminalStore.ts` — sessions, active session
- `TerminalInstance.tsx` — xterm.js with FitAddon, themed to match app
- `TerminalTabs.tsx` — tab bar with + button
- `TerminalPanel.tsx` — composes tabs + terminal
- IPC data flow: pty.onData → IPC → xterm.write (and reverse)

### Step 7: Status Bar
- `StatusBar.tsx` — green "Ready" dot, file count, cursor position, encoding
- Connect to editor store for cursor pos, file tree store for item count

### Step 8: Pixel Art Mascot
- Sprite sheet approach: 8-frame idle animation, 64x64 per frame
- `useSpriteAnimation.ts` — CSS `steps()` animation on background-position
- `MascotWidget.tsx` — fixed position bottom-right, `image-rendering: pixelated`
- Scale 2x for visibility, `pointer-events: none`

### Step 9: Keyboard Shortcuts + Polish
- `useKeyboardShortcuts.ts` — Ctrl+S, Ctrl+W, Ctrl+B (sidebar), Ctrl+` (terminal)
- `menu.ts` — native Electron menu (File, Edit, View, Terminal, Help)
- `windowState.ts` — save/restore window position
- Hover transitions, scroll styling, focus rings

## IPC Architecture
```
Renderer (React)  ←contextBridge→  Preload  ←ipcRenderer.invoke→  Main (Node)
   window.api.fs.readFile(path)  →  ipc  →  fs/promises.readFile(path)
   window.api.terminal.create()  →  ipc  →  ptyManager.create(shell, cwd)
   terminal:data event           ←  ipc  ←  pty.onData(data)
```

## Key Dependencies
```
electron ^33, electron-vite ^2.4, react ^18.3, typescript ^5.6
monaco-editor ^0.52, @monaco-editor/react ^4.7
@xterm/xterm ^5.5, @xterm/addon-fit ^0.10, node-pty ^1.0
zustand ^5.0, react-resizable-panels ^2.1, lucide-react ^0.468
```

## Verification
1. `npm run dev` launches the IDE with dark purple theme
2. Open a folder → file tree populates in sidebar
3. Click a file → opens in Monaco editor with syntax highlighting
4. Multiple tabs work (open, switch, close, modified indicator)
5. Terminal panel opens with working shell (PowerShell on Windows)
6. Panels resize via drag handles
7. Toolbar buttons render with correct colors (green/orange)
8. Tools dropdown opens with categorized placeholder menu
9. Mascot animates in bottom-right corner
10. Ctrl+S saves, Ctrl+B toggles sidebar, Ctrl+` toggles terminal
11. Window position persists across restarts

If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: C:\Users\koalo\.claude\projects\C--Users-koalo-Desktop-MeNoach-IDE\2ad6d269-20e1-4da4-afbe-8a045f6d1eba.jsonl
I'll start implementing the MeNoach IDE MVP. This is a large project, so I'll work through it systematically.
Run: ls -la "/c/Users/koalo/Desktop/MeNoach IDE/" 2>/dev/null || echo "Empty or doesn't exist"
Empty directory. Let me scaffold the Electron project and build everything step by step. I'll create tasks to track progress and work through them.
Now let me create all the source directories and files. I'll work on multiple files in parallel where possible.
Run: cd "/c/Users/koalo/Desktop/MeNoach IDE" && mkdir -p resources/mascot src/main/ipc src/main/services src/preload src/rend
Run: npm view electron-vite versions --json 2>/dev/null | tail -5
Run: npm view electron-rebuild versions --json 2>/dev/null | tail -5
electron-vite is now at v5. Let me also check a few other package versions:
Run: npm view electron-vite@latest version && npm view @electron-toolkit/tsconfig@latest version && npm view monaco-editor@la
node-pty needs Visual Studio Build Tools for compilation. Let me check what's available and adapt:
Run: npm view node-pty-prebuilt-multiarch version 2>/dev/null || echo "not found"
Run: where cl 2>/dev/null; where python 2>/dev/null; npm config get msvs_version 2>/dev/null
No VS Build Tools. I'll remove the postinstall, install deps, then handle node-pty with a fallback approach.
Run: cd "/c/Users/koalo/Desktop/MeNoach IDE" && rm -rf node_modules package-lock.json && npm install 2>&1

Dependencies installed. I'll use `child_process.spawn` as a fallback for the terminal instead of node-pty (works without native compilation). Now let me build everything. I'll create all the files systematically.
### Step 2: Theme system and global styles
### Step 5: Renderer — types, utils, stores, hooks

### Step 6: UI Components

Now for the big piece — all the React components. I'll create them all:
Now I need a missing dependency — `@electron-toolkit/utils` is referenced in main but not installed. Let me check:
Only `@electron-toolkit/tsconfig` is installed. I need `@electron-toolkit/utils` too. Let me install it:
Run: cd "/c/Users/koalo/Desktop/MeNoach IDE" && npx electron-vite build 2>&1
Build succeeds. Let me now try running dev mode to make sure it launches:
Run: cd "/c/Users/koalo/Desktop/MeNoach IDE" && timeout 20 npx electron-vite dev 2>&1 || true
The app compiles and launches. Let me also add a keyframe for the spinner animation used in the file tree, and ensure the `@keyframes spin` is defined:
Now let me check there are no import issues I may have missed. Let me verify the electron-vite config handles `@electron-toolkit/utils` correctly:
Run: cd "/c/Users/koalo/Desktop/MeNoach IDE" && npx electron-vite build 2>&1

Everything builds successfully. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## MeNoach IDE MVP — Implementation Complete

### What was built:

**Main Process** (`src/main/`)
- `index.ts` — Electron app lifecycle, BrowserWindow creation
- `menu.ts` — Native menu (File, Edit, View, Terminal, Help)
- `windowState.ts` — Persists window position/size across restarts
- `ipc/fileSystem.ts` — Read directory (recursive), read/write files, stat
- `ipc/terminal.ts` — Terminal sessions using `child_process.spawn` (fallback for node-pty since VS Build Tools aren't available)
- `ipc/dialog.ts` — Native open folder/file/save dialogs
- `services/fileWatcher.ts` — Chokidar file watcher with IPC notifications

**Preload** (`src/preload/`)
- `index.ts` — contextBridge exposing `window.api` (fs, terminal, dialog, menu events)
- `api.d.ts` — Full TypeScript type definitions

**Renderer** (`src/renderer/`)
- **Stores**: workspaceStore, fileTreeStore, editorStore, terminalStore, layoutStore, uiStore (all Zustand)
- **Hooks**: useFileSystem, useTerminal, useKeyboardShortcuts, useMenuEvents
- **Layout**: AppLayout (CSS Grid), PanelLayout (react-resizable-panels with purple accent resize handles)
- **Toolbar**: AppLogo, ToolsDropdown (green, categorized mega-menu), QuickAccessButtons (orange Claude/Codex), LayoutToggle (3 modes), WorkspaceSelector
- **Sidebar**: FileExplorer with filter, FileTree + FileTreeItem (recursive, with file type icons/colors)
- **Editor**: Monaco Editor with custom `menoach-dark` theme, tabs with modified indicators, breadcrumbs, welcome screen
- **Terminal**: xterm.js panels with themed colors, tabbed sessions, auto-create first terminal
- **StatusBar**: Ready indicator, file count, cursor position, language, encoding
- **Mascot**: Pixel art owl (SVG) with bobbing animation, bottom-right corner

**Theme**: Full dark purple color system via CSS custom properties

### To run:
```bash
cd "MeNoach IDE"
npm run dev
```

### Note on terminal:
The terminal uses `child_process.spawn` instead of `node-pty` since Visual Studio Build Tools aren't installed. If you install VS Build Tools later, you can add `node-pty` back with `npm install node-pty` and run `npm run rebuild-pty`, then update `src/main/ipc/terminal.ts` to use the PTY for full terminal emulation (resize support, etc.).
Fantastic. Can we run it again?

Run: cd "/c/Users/koalo/Desktop/MeNoach IDE" && npx electron-vite dev 2>&1 &
sleep 15 && kill %1 2>/dev/null; wait 2>/dev/nul

The app launched successfully — compiled main process, preload, and renderer, then started the Electron window. It was running on `localhost:5174` (5173 was in use).

If you want to keep it running interactively, run `npm run dev` directly from your terminal in the `MeNoach IDE` folder. That way you can interact with the window, open folders, use the editor, etc.
I wasn't able to type anything in the terminal.

The issue is that `child_process.spawn` with piped stdio doesn't create a pseudo-terminal, so the shell doesn't echo input back. I need to handle input echoing manually. Let me fix both the main process terminal handler and the renderer component.
The root cause: without a PTY, the shell doesn't echo typed characters back. I need to handle line buffering and local echo in the renderer. Let me rewrite the terminal instance to do this properly.
I also need to fix the backspace handling — let me simplify it:
The backspace logic is overly complex. Let me simplify it:
Now let me also fix the main process to send output to all windows (not just the focused one, which can be null):
Run: cd "/c/Users/koalo/Desktop/MeNoach IDE" && npx electron-vite build 2>&1
Clean build. Now let's launch it:

Run: cd "/c/Users/koalo/Desktop/MeNoach IDE" && npx electron-vite dev 2>&1 &
sleep 15 && kill %1 2>/dev/null; wait 2>/dev/nul

It's running. Here's what changed:

**The fix:** The terminal now handles **local echo** since `child_process.spawn` (without a PTY) doesn't echo input back to the terminal. The `TerminalInstance` component now:

- **Buffers typed characters** and echoes them locally to xterm
- **Enter** sends the buffered line + newline to the process
- **Backspace** deletes from the buffer and visually erases the character
- **Ctrl+C** sends the interrupt signal and clears the buffer
- **Left/Right arrows** move the cursor within the line buffer
- **Home/End** jump to start/end of input
- **Ctrl+U** clears the current line
- **Ctrl+L** clears the screen

Also fixed the main process to broadcast terminal output to all windows (not just the focused one, which could be null).

Run `npm run dev` from your terminal to try it — you should now be able to type commands and see them echoed as you type.
It doesn't seem like it's operating as a normal terminal window at the bottom, and I'm also not able to copy the text from what it's saying. Post it in here, I'll send a picture over.

I can see two issues from the screenshot:

1. **Double echo** — typed text appears twice (once from local echo, once from cmd.exe's echo)
2. **Can't copy text** — the `user-select: none` on body is blocking it

Let me fix both.

OK, here's the plan:

1. **Double echo fix**: Spawn cmd.exe with `/Q` flag (turns off command echo). cmd.exe still displays its output and prompts, but won't re-echo the command we typed. Our local echo handles showing the typed text.
2. **Copy fix**: Allow `user-select` inside the terminal container.

Let me test what `/Q` actually does — it suppresses the command echo but the prompt should still appear:
Now fix the terminal instance — add echo stripping as a safety net and fix copy support:
Now fix the global CSS so `user-select: none` doesn't bleed into the terminal:
Run: cd "/c/Users/koalo/Desktop/MeNoach IDE" && npx electron-vite build 2>&1

Clean build. Here's what I changed:

**Double echo fix:**
- cmd.exe now spawns with `/Q /K` flags — `/Q` turns off command echo (cmd.exe won't repeat what you type), `/K` keeps it interactive
- Our local echo in xterm still shows characters as you type
- Now you'll see: `C:\path>hello` (your typing) then just the command output, no duplicate "hello"

**Copy fix:**
- Added `user-select: text` to the terminal container and `.xterm` / `.monaco-editor` classes in global CSS
- Added `Ctrl+Shift+C` to copy selected text, `Ctrl+Shift+V` to paste
- Enabled `rightClickSelectsWord` in xterm options

Run `npm run dev` to try it out.

Yeah, I really want there to be like a window tile system that it gets easier to click and drag, like make the window small, like as you make one of them smaller, the other one stays, the other one gets bigger. or just a way of like tile management. When you have, I wanna be able to run multiple terminals and they all kind of work neatly together. Because right now, the way I'm doing it through just using regular command prompt terminals, it gets very hard to manage.

Now I can see the QuadCode layout — it's a tiled window manager where Issues, Terminal 2, Worker terminal, and Ideas all sit as independent draggable/resizable tiles in a grid. Let me explore what we have and plan this out.

[subagent] Explore the MeNoach IDE project at "C:\Users\koalo\Desktop\MeNoach IDE" with medium thoroughness. I need to understand:

1. The current layout system - how panels are arranged (PanelLayout.tsx, AppLayout.tsx)
2. The current terminal panel implementation (TerminalPanel.tsx, TerminalTabs.tsx, TerminalInstance.tsx)
3. How react-resizable-panels is currently used
4. The layout store (layoutStore.ts)
5. The terminal store (terminalStore.ts)

Read all these files and summarize their structure and how they interconnect.
Good, I have a clear picture of the current system. Now let me look at the QuadCode screenshot more carefully and plan the tile system.

[subagent] I need to design a tiled window management system for MeNoach IDE (Electron + React + TypeScript app). The user showed me a screenshot of QuadCode IDE which has this feature.

**What QuadCode shows:**
- The main content area (between toolbar and statusbar) is a grid of independent "tile" panels
- Each tile has: a colored title bar (with label like "Issues", "Terminal 2", "Worker"), close button (X), and content area
- Tiles are arranged in a flexible grid - in the screenshot there are 4 tiles: Issues (left), Terminal 2 (center-left), Worker terminal (center-right), Ideas (right)
- Below those, Terminal 4 spans the full width at the bottom
- Tiles can be resized by dragging borders between them
- Making one tile smaller makes adjacent tiles bigger
- Multiple terminals run simultaneously as separate tiles (not just tabs within one terminal panel)
- Non-terminal panels (Issues, Ideas) sit alongside terminals

**Current MeNoach IDE architecture:**
- Uses `react-resizable-panels` for layout (PanelLayout.tsx)
- Current layout is fixed: sidebar | (editor / terminal) — vertical split within horizontal split
- Terminal sessions exist in terminalStore but render as tabs within a single TerminalPanel
- Layout modes: 'default', 'zen', 'terminal-focus' in layoutStore

**The goal:**
Replace the rigid sidebar|editor|terminal layout with a flexible tiled window manager where:
1. Users can have multiple tiles open simultaneously (terminals, editor, future panels like Issues/Ideas)
2. Tiles arrange in a grid — horizontal and vertical splits
3. Dragging borders between tiles resizes them (one shrinks, adjacent grows)
4. Each tile has a title bar with label and close button
5. Users can split existing tiles (split right, split down) to create new ones
6. Closing a tile makes adjacent tiles expand to fill the space
7. The sidebar (file explorer) remains as a fixed panel on the left (not part of the tile system)
8. The editor and terminals become tile types

**Technical constraints:**
- Must use react-resizable-panels (already installed)
- Must work with existing Zustand stores
- Must integrate with existing terminal IPC

**Please design:**
1. A data model for the tile tree (nested splits)
2. A Zustand store for managing the tile layout
3. Component hierarchy for rendering tiles
4. How to add/remove/split tiles
5. How different tile types (editor, terminal) render within the tile system
6. How the existing EditorArea and TerminalInstance components fit in
Excellent design. Let me write the final plan.
[Request interrupted by user for tool use]
